title: Mechanism Step Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Animated two-step mechanism showing intermediate buildup and concentration changes as reactants collide, form C, and regenerate A.

learningObjectives: Visualize how rate-determining steps cause intermediates to accumulate.
Track concentrations of reactants, intermediates, and products during a catalytic cycle.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive animation with live concentration bars
keyQuestions: What happens to intermediate C when step 1 is slow versus fast?
How does regenerating A affect the overall mechanism?
commonMisconceptions: Intermediates and catalysts are the same
The rate-determining step is always the last step
assessmentOpportunities: Students document concentration changes for two slider settings
Learners explain the animation in terms of collision theory
